narrative: Friday morning in Billings, late March, and it’s colder than yesterday — twenty-eight degrees at dawn with frost thick on the windshields. The sky is that particular Montana gray that could go either way: clear by noon or build into something. Downtown, Amber Tuck is standing in her bookstore with wet shoes and a phone pressed to her ear, trying to find a plumber before the water reaches the poetry section. Jolene is at the coffee shop two blocks away, hands around a mug, across from a face she last saw at seventeen. The rest of the city moves through its Friday rhythms — Dani already has a transmission up on the lift at Heights Auto, Nora is three hours into a shift at St. Vincent, and Marcus is coming off his overnight at the refinery, driving home with the windows cracked against the tiredness.
events: A pipe bursts at Tuck’s Books overnight — Amber arrives to find an inch of water across the back room, destroying three boxes of unsorted inventory
Jolene stops at the downtown coffee shop and runs into a woman she hasn’t seen since high school — Megan Calloway, who left Billings at eighteen and apparently came back
